title: Chapter 13 - Form Basics
description: Exercises on basic form elements and labels.

questions.prompt: Which tag groups form controls for submission?

explanations.overview: <form> wraps controls and can be submitted to a server.

byOptionId.form: Correct — <form> groups inputs and submission behavior.
byOptionId.fieldset: Incorrect — <fieldset> groups related controls but doesn't enable submission on its own.
byOptionId.input: Incorrect — <input> is a control inside a form, not the form container.
byOptionId.label: Incorrect — <label> labels a control but isn't the form container.

options.label: To improve accessibility and connect text to controls
It's optional and has no benefit

explanations.overview: Labels help screen readers and allow clicking the text to focus the control.

byOptionId.access: Correct — <label> improves accessibility and focus behavior.
byOptionId.style: Incorrect — styling is done via CSS, not the label element itself.
byOptionId.submit: Incorrect — <label> does not submit forms automatically.
byOptionId.none: Incorrect — labels provide accessibility benefits and should be used.
